results.abstract: <jats:p>Artificial intelligence is profoundly influencing various facets of our lives, indicating its potential to significantly impact sustainability. Nevertheless, capturing the productivity gains stemming from artificial intelligence in macro-level data poses challenges, leading to the question of whether artificial intelligence is reminiscent of the “Solow paradox”. This study employs micro-level manufacturing data to investigate the impact of artificial intelligence on firms’ productivity.

results.title: The aggregation paradox of AI
results.abstract: <jats:p>This research brief examines why strong productivity gains from artificial intelligence (AI) observed at the task and individual worker level have not yet translated into measurable productivity growth at the firm, sectoral or macroeconomic level. It reviews emerging evidence on AI adoption, diffusion and workplace transformation, while exploring the economic and institutional conditions that shape how productivity gains scale across the economy.</jats:p>

results.title: Does AI Enhance or Replace Human Productivity? Evidence from Service and Manufacturing Sectors
results.venue: International Journal of Multidisciplinary Research and Growth Evaluation
results.tldr: Evidence from service-sector experiments shows that AI can substantially increase productivity in customer service, professional writing, software development, consulting, and administrative work, particularly when tasks are structured, information-intensive, and supported by clear performance metrics, but gains are uneven and depend on worker skill, task type, data quality, organizational redesign, and human judgment.
results.abstract: Evidence from service-sector experiments shows that AI can substantially increase productivity in customer service, professional writing, software development, consulting, and administrative work, particularly when tasks are structured, information-intensive, and supported by clear performance metrics, but gains are uneven and depend on worker skill, task type, data quality, organizational redesign, and human judgment.

results.abstract: This study investigates how mechatronic sensors and actuators enhance precision, energy efficiency, and productivity in automotive manufacturing and control systems by integrating real-time data acquisition, adaptive control algorithms, and embedded actuator-sensor feedback loops.

LLM-Driven Lean Manufacturing System: Integrating AI, RAG, and Embeddings for Industry 4.0 Decision Support
results.venue: Journal of Computer Science and Technology Studies
results.tldr: This comprehensive research report presents the architecture, design rationale, and empirical evaluation of a web-based Lean Manufacturing System (LMS) that employs LLMs to automate the generation of structured Lean tools, including Value Stream Mapping (VSM), 5W2H action planning, Ishikawa diagrams, Pareto analysis, and GUT prioritization matrices.
results.abstract: This comprehensive research report presents the architecture, design rationale, and empirical evaluation of a web-based Lean Manufacturing System (LMS) that employs LLMs to automate the generation of structured Lean tools, including Value Stream Mapping (VSM), 5W2H action planning, Ishikawa diagrams, Pareto analysis, and GUT prioritization matrices.

results.title: De la Automatización a la Autonomía: Consecuencias de la Inteligencia Artificial en la Cuarta Revolución Industrial
results.tldr: A critical view on the future of work in the AI era is provided, highlighting the importance of a regulatory framework that protects workers and fosters a balanced technological integration across sectors.
results.abstract: A critical view on the future of work in the AI era is provided, highlighting the importance of a regulatory framework that protects workers and fosters a balanced technological integration across sectors.

results.title: DIGITAL TWIN TECHNOLOGY FOR INTELLIGENT MANUFACTURING: A COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW OF INDUSTRIAL APPLICATIONS, CHALLENGES, AND FUTURE OPPORTUNITIES
results.tldr: The findings indicate that Digital Twin technology has the potential to transform manufacturing operations by improving productivity, reducing operational costs, enhancing product quality, and supporting sustainable industrial development.
results.abstract: The findings indicate that Digital Twin technology has the potential to transform manufacturing operations by improving productivity, reducing operational costs, enhancing product quality, and supporting sustainable industrial development.

results.title: Economics of labor in the digital economy.
results.venue: Journal of management, economics and technology
results.tldr: The digital economy is reshaping labor markets, creating both opportunities and challenges, and addressing these changes requires a comprehensive approach, including investments in education, social protection, and digital infrastructure.
results.abstract: The digital economy is reshaping labor markets, creating both opportunities and challenges, and addressing these changes requires a comprehensive approach, including investments in education, social protection, and digital infrastructure.

results.title: AI-Driven Automation: Transforming Industry 5.0 withMachine Learning and Advanced Technologies
Enhancing operational excellence in manufacturing through cloudification: A framework for optimizing efficiency and innovation in industrial processes
results.venue: World Journal of Advanced Engineering Technology and Sciences
results.tldr: It is demonstrated how cloud-based Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), IoT-enabled products, and AI-driven analytics can significantly increase output and foster creativity in manufacturing.
results.abstract: It is demonstrated how cloud-based Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), IoT-enabled products, and AI-driven analytics can significantly increase output and foster creativity in manufacturing.

results.title: Green artificial intelligence adoption in industrial systems: A SWOT assessment of opportunities and challenges
results.venue: International Journal of Applied Resilience and Sustainability
results.tldr: A SWOT analysis of the green implementation of artificial intelligence in industrial systems under the scope of the current urgent demand on environmentally friendly AI implementation patterns indicates that organizational willingness, technological readiness, and regulatory standards all contribute positively to the difference in the levels of green AI adoption.
results.abstract: A SWOT analysis of the green implementation of artificial intelligence in industrial systems under the scope of the current urgent demand on environmentally friendly AI implementation patterns indicates that organizational willingness, technological readiness, and regulatory standards all contribute positively to the difference in the levels of green AI adoption.

results.title: AI and Automation: Effects on Employment and Management
results.tldr: A typology of employment impact, ranging from automation-intensive displacement to augmentation-driven productivity gains, is proposed, arguing that the future of work depends not only on technological capability but on proactive policy, inclusive design, and agile management strategies.
results.abstract: A typology of employment impact, ranging from automation-intensive displacement to augmentation-driven productivity gains, is proposed, arguing that the future of work depends not only on technological capability but on proactive policy, inclusive design, and agile management strategies.

results.title: Industrial Artificial Intelligence and Urban Carbon Reduction: Evidence from Chinese Cities
results.tldr: The findings suggest that intelligent industrial upgrading can simultaneously enhance productivity and support climate mitigation, but this effect is highly context-dependent, offering policy insights for achieving sustainable industrial modernization and carbon neutrality in emerging economies.
results.abstract: The findings suggest that intelligent industrial upgrading can simultaneously enhance productivity and support climate mitigation, but this effect is highly context-dependent, offering policy insights for achieving sustainable industrial modernization and carbon neutrality in emerging economies.

results.title: Smart Metallurgy: The Impact of Industry 4.0 on Materials Processing and Innovation
results.venue: The Annals of "Dunarea de Jos" University of Galati. Fascicle IX, Metallurgy and Materials Science
results.tldr: The function of enabling technologies such as Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), Additive Manufacturing (AM) and Big Data analytics are highlighted, revealing their cumulative ability to enhance energy efficiency, resource allocation, quality control, and predictive maintenance along the whole metallurgical value chain.
results.abstract: The function of enabling technologies such as Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), Additive Manufacturing (AM) and Big Data analytics are highlighted, revealing their cumulative ability to enhance energy efficiency, resource allocation, quality control, and predictive maintenance along the whole metallurgical value chain.

results.title: Researcher Shaping the Future of Intelligent Engineering
results.tldr: Ivan Polshchikov's work represents a model for 21st-century engineering—systemic, environmentally conscious, and economically resilient—linking academic rigor with industrial applicability.
results.abstract: Ivan Polshchikov's work represents a model for 21st-century engineering—systemic, environmentally conscious, and economically resilient—linking academic rigor with industrial applicability.

results.title: Automation and Artificial Intelligence in Labor Markets: Analyzing Trends, Transitions, and Transformations
results.tldr: The paper analyzes policy responses to the changing labor market like universal basic income, sponsored retraining programs, and strengthening labor protections by looking through industry specific case studies of how different sectors have assimilated to the rise of AI.
results.abstract: The paper analyzes policy responses to the changing labor market like universal basic income, sponsored retraining programs, and strengthening labor protections by looking through industry specific case studies of how different sectors have assimilated to the rise of AI.

AI Factories as Enterprise Intelligence Infrastructure: How GPU-Powered Systems Are Redefining Value Creation across Industries

results.tldr: Examination of AI factories as enterprise intelligence infrastructure and how high-performance computing architectures are redefining value creation across multiple industries positions AI factories as scalable intelligence platforms capable of converting computational power into sustained innovation, measurable productivity, and resilient competitive advantage across complex digital ecosystems globally and.

results.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)

results.title: Artificial intelligence industry development fortifies regional economic resilience: evidence from Chinese cities
results.tldr: The empirical results indicate that every 1% increase in the number of AI-related firms in China’s prefectures leads to the increase of regional economic resilience, and it is demonstrated that AI industry development is associated with higher urban economic productivity, industrial diversification and labor supply resilience.
results.abstract: The empirical results indicate that every 1% increase in the number of AI-related firms in China’s prefectures leads to the increase of regional economic resilience, and it is demonstrated that AI industry development is associated with higher urban economic productivity, industrial diversification and labor supply resilience.

Essays on development economics and industrial organization
RePAIR Lab: Hybrid Intelligence for Human-Centered, Sustainable Remanufacturing of Electronics following a Human-Technology-Organization approach
results.venue: IOP Conference Series: Materials Science and Engineering
results.tldr: The approach reframes remanufacturing as a socio-technical system where circularity, digital transformation, and human-centered design converge-advancing sustainable, human-centered manufacturing and bridging the gap between the ambitions of a circular economy and industrial practice.
results.abstract: The approach reframes remanufacturing as a socio-technical system where circularity, digital transformation, and human-centered design converge-advancing sustainable, human-centered manufacturing and bridging the gap between the ambitions of a circular economy and industrial practice.

results.title: A taxonomy of artificial intelligence techniques for machining parameter optimization
results.venue: Multiscale and Multidisciplinary Modeling Experiments and Design
results.tldr: A novel taxonomy is established that classifies AI techniques along three dimensions: learning paradigm, optimization scope, and implementation mode, and reveals that hybrid AI models, which synergistically combine multiple algorithms, consistently achieve superior performance.
results.abstract: A novel taxonomy is established that classifies AI techniques along three dimensions: learning paradigm, optimization scope, and implementation mode, and reveals that hybrid AI models, which synergistically combine multiple algorithms, consistently achieve superior performance.

results.title: Microbial Enzyme Production: Critical Bottlenecks and Integrated Engineering Solutions.
results.tldr: Comparing analysis across major enzyme classes-cellulases, proteases, lipases, lipases, and amylases-reveals both universal constraints and enzyme-specific production challenges, which provide practical guidance for translating laboratory advances into economically viable microbial enzyme production systems.
results.abstract: Comparing analysis across major enzyme classes-cellulases, proteases, lipases, lipases, and amylases-reveals both universal constraints and enzyme-specific production challenges, which provide practical guidance for translating laboratory advances into economically viable microbial enzyme production systems.
